top_entities.name: El Salvador
top_entities.news_summary: CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENT REMOVES TERM LIMITS (July 2026): Legislative Assembly amended constitution allowing indefinite presidential re-election. Cristosal human rights organization suspended El Salvador operations citing "escalating repression" — executive director: "had to choose between prison and exile." Anti-corruption chief Ruth Lopez arrested May. 140+ human rights defenders/journalists fled May-September. 86 political prisoners. Torture, enforced disappearances, due process violations documented.

top_entities.name: China
top_entities.news_summary: ETHNIC UNITY LAW DAY 2 + TAIWAN MILITARY PRESSURE (Jul 2): Al Jazeera July 2 report: global condemnation mounting; Taiwan announcing counter-measures. Article 63 extraterritorial reach active — diaspora Uyghur/Tibetan communities now legally liable under Chinese law. China Coast Guard patrolling Taiwan's eastern EEZ continuously since June 1. Taiwan commissioned Littoral Combat Command July 1. China-Japan tensions: China restricting dual-use/rare earth exports.

top_entities.name: Lebanon
top_entities.news_summary: POST-CEASEFIRE FRAGILITY CONTINUING (Jul 2): US-brokered June 26 ceasefire framework rejected by Hezbollah (demands full Israeli withdrawal vs framework tying it to Hezbollah disarmament). IDF operations continuing. Doha talks include Lebanon stabilization on agenda. 4,000+ killed since Mar 2; 1.2M+ displaced. More than 1 in 6 Lebanese displaced. Fragile situation could collapse rapidly.

top_entities.name: Nigeria
top_entities.news_summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK — CONTINUING (Jul 2): 34.8M at IPC Phase 3+ (highest-ever recorded). 15,000 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno. June-September lean season at peak severity. FAO-WFP Hunger Hotspots: highest-concern global hotspot. 52.8M food insecure across West Africa/Sahel. JNIM cross-border attacks escalating from Sahel into northern Nigeria. WFP needs $129M; only 1 in 10 reached.

top_entities.name: Pakistan
top_entities.news_summary: MAJOR FLOODING (late June 2026): 6.9M people affected; 1,000+ deaths; 3.5M+ displaced. Khyber Pakhtunkhwa disaster response deemed inadequate. Afghan refugee deportations accelerating in July targeting PoR cardholders despite refugee status. Online speech law amendments enabling detention of journalists/activists. UNICEF statement on flash floods current within window.

top_entities.name: Venezuela
top_entities.news_summary: EARTHQUAKE DAY 8 (Jul 2): Death toll 2,595 (up from 1,943 Jul 1); 50,000 unaccounted; 12,400+ injured. Rescuer pulled survivor from collapsed mall after 120-hour operation. 862+ aftershocks; water system failed in some areas. 20+ country rescue teams. US $300M commitment. Government response failures documented: VP Rodriguez blamed for haphazard coordination. UNICEF second shipment completed.

top_entities.name: Taiwan
top_entities.news_summary: MILITARY PRESSURE + DEFENSE RESPONSE (Jul 1-2, within window): Taiwan commissioned Littoral Combat Command July 1 integrating US/Taiwanese missile systems. China Coast Guard continuously patrolling eastern EEZ since June 1. Taiwan conducted quarantine tabletop exercises June 25. Taiwan counter-measures to China Ethnic Unity Law announced. Touched by taiwan-china-japan-security-batch.

top_entities.name: Princeton University
top_entities.news_summary: FEDERAL FUNDING DISPUTE (within window): Princeton President Eisgruber vowed to defend academic freedom. Part of Ivy League coalition. Touched by universities-batch.

top_entities.name: Mali
top_entities.news_summary: JNIM SIEGE + LEAN SEASON (ongoing within window): 5M in need of humanitarian aid; 1.56M acutely food insecure; pockets of famine-like conditions in northern Mali. JNIM April 25 2026 coordinated attacks on Bamako, Gao, Kati, Kidal — largest offensive in Mali War since 2012. Siege warfare restricting food/fuel/medicine access across major regions. Humanitarian response only 18.5% funded. 55M people at crisis-level hunger across West/Central Africa June-August.

top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.news_summary: MASSIVE KYIV ATTACK (Jul 1-2): Russia struck Kyiv with 70+ missiles and ~500 drones; 27+ killed, 100+ injured, every district hit. 52,500 sheltered in metro including 4,500 children. UN: rescuers working to save trapped residents. 3.7M internally displaced; 10.8M requiring humanitarian assistance in 2026. Civilian toll at highest monthly level in three years. Touched by eastern-europe-conflict-batch.

top_entities.name: Georgia
top_entities.news_summary: DAY 581-582 PROTESTS (Jul 1-2): Continuous protests in Tbilisi since November 2024 demanding open elections and release of political prisoners. Activists Shervashidze and Toshkhua released from detention July 1 after sentences softened. Protesters continue demanding release of Nana Sander and Zviad Tsetskhladze. Police used tear gas/water cannons historically. Government cracking down on civic space.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.news_summary: DOJ CRIMINAL + CIVIL PROBE — CONTINUING (Jul 2): Criminal probe extends to Optum Rx and physician reimbursement. Civil probe: inflated diagnoses to trigger higher Medicare Advantage payments. Third-party review ongoing. Q2 earnings call July 29. Senate investigation: UnitedHealth "aggressively gaming" Medicare Advantage. DOJ June 23 healthcare fraud takedown ($6.5B, 455 defendants) maintains sector scrutiny.

top_entities.name: Bahrain
top_entities.news_summary: DOHA POSITIVE PROGRESS (Jul 2): US Fifth Fleet HQ in Bahrain; Doha talks concluded positively today. GCC civilian recovery from Iranian strikes ongoing. 28+ GCC civilians killed from cumulative strikes during war. Amnesty International June 2026: Iranian drone strikes on Bahrain may constitute war crimes. IMO: 14 seafarer deaths. Bahrain never assessed.

top_entities.name: Burkina Faso
top_entities.news_summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK + JNIM SIEGE (Jul 2, ongoing): 52.8M food insecure across West Africa/Sahel (June-August peak). Burkina Faso #1 globally for terrorism (GTI 2026). 2.1M+ IDPs — largest in Central Sahel. JNIM besieged population 3.5M+. 28% of $3.7B appeal funded. 401,000 children with severe acute malnutrition. Military dissolved 118 NGOs April 15. Civic space collapsed. No new specific July 2 development beyond ongoing severity.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of C
top_entities.news_summary: EBOLA OUTBREAK UPDATE (Jun 29-Jul 1): As of June 29: 1,333 confirmed cases / 399 deaths (up 26 cases/22 deaths vs prior day). 609 patients hospitalized. Haut-Uele (4th province, bordering South Sudan/CAR) confirmed June 29. France imported case June 24. Uganda 20 cases/2 deaths. No approved vaccine/treatment for Bundibugyo strain. ReliefWeb Situation Report #9 dated July 1. UNHCR seeking $14M for preparedness.

sector_alerts.title: Russia massive Kyiv attack July 1-2: 27+ killed, 100+ injured — 70 missiles and ~500 drones
sector_alerts.summary: Overnight July 1-2 2026: Russia launched 70+ missiles (nearly half ballistic) and ~500 drones at Kyiv — one of the largest single attacks on the Ukrainian capital. At least 27 killed, 100+ injured. Every district of Kyiv hit. A 9-floor residential building in Darnytskyi district partially collapsed. Kyiv metro sheltered a record 52,500 people including 4,500 children. Energy infrastructure damaged; some areas without electricity. European leaders repledged support to Ukraine. Russia framed as retaliation for Ukrainian strikes on oil facilities. Ukraine: this continues pattern of deliberate civilian targeting with 62,716 casualties since Feb 2022.

sector_alerts.title: Sudan RSF advances on El Obeid (North Kordofan): UN warns of "impending atrocities"; Amnesty confirms El Fasher "crimes against humanity"
sector_alerts.summary: July 2, 2026: RSF escalating drone strikes on El Obeid (capital of North Kordofan state), killing civilians and cutting off food, water, and fuel. IRC warns of imminent humanitarian catastrophe mirroring the El Fasher siege pattern. UN Human Rights Chief Turk: "Stop this madness" — warning of catastrophic civilian impact. Separately, Amnesty International released a July 2026 report documenting RSF "crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing" at El Fasher from May 2024-October 2025. 28.9M people acutely food insecure in Sudan; more people in famine in Sudan than rest of world combined. The El Obeid offensive represents a potential new siege front in the Sudanese civil war.

sector_alerts.title: Venezuela earthquake Day 8: death toll 2,595 (up 652 from Day 7); 50,000 unaccounted
sector_alerts.summary: July 2, 2026 (Day 8 since June 24 twin earthquakes): Death toll confirmed at 2,595, up from 1,943 on July 1 — a 34% single-day increase. Nearly 50,000 people remain unaccounted. 12,400+ injured. 862+ aftershocks. Water system failed in some affected areas. Miraculous rescues continuing: man pulled from rubble of collapsed mall after 120-hour rescue. Government response failures persist: VP Rodriguez still blamed for haphazard coordination. Venezuela lacks ambulances, heavy machinery. 20+ country rescue teams operating. US commitment $300M.

sector_alerts.title: Iran-US Doha talks Day 2: "positive progress" — Hormuz MOU on track; nuclear framework emerging
sector_alerts.scope: countries/iran, countries/kuwait, countries/bahrain, countries/qatar, countries/saudi-arabia, countries/uae, countries/oman
sector_alerts.summary: July 2, 2026: Qatar reports "positive progress" as US-Iran indirect technical talks concluded in Doha. Trump: "very good meetings." Vance: "going well" and "discussions on nuclear issue will begin." No new strikes July 1-2 from either side. Hormuz toll dispute remains hardest open issue — Vance rejected any Iranian toll mechanism; Iran asserts joint Hormuz sovereignty with Oman. All GCC civilian recovery underway from months of strikes. 1,000+ ships and 20,000 crew stranded at peak of crisis; flows slowly recovering. UAE state oil company: full Hormuz flows not until 2027.

sector_alerts.title: EU AI Act Digital Omnibus: high-risk AI deadline deferred from August 2, 2026 to December 2, 2027
sector_alerts.summary: July 2, 2026: The EU Digital Omnibus provisional agreement (reached May 7, endorsed by EU Parliament June 16) is expected to be formally adopted and published in the Official Journal in July 2026. Key change: Annex III high-risk AI system obligations are deferred from August 2, 2026 to December 2, 2027 (16-month delay). Prohibited practices (since February 2025) and GPAI rules (since August 2025) remain enforceable unchanged. This materially changes the compliance landscape compared to the prior scan context (which cited August 2 as 32 days away). GPAI Code signatories include: Amazon, Anthropic, Google, IBM, Microsoft, Mistral, Cohere, Samsung. Meta declined to sign. xAI signed safety chapter only.

sector_alerts.title: El Salvador: constitutional amendment removes presidential term limits July 2026; rights organization closes citing repression
sector_alerts.summary: July 2026: El Salvador Legislative Assembly (controlled by ruling party) amended constitution to allow indefinite presidential re-election for Bukele. Concurrently, Cristosal — a leading human rights organization — suspended operations in El Salvador. Executive director says team "had to choose between prison and exile." Ruth Lopez (anti-corruption chief) was arrested in May. Between May and September 2026, at least 140 human rights defenders and journalists have fled the country. 86 current political prisoners. Year 5 of Bukele state of exception. Documented: mass arbitrary detention, enforced disappearance, torture, due process violations.
